history: The client is a PhD candidate engaged in scientific research, working on a project with a senior scientist who has struggled to gain recognition. They have perfectionistic tendencies and often feel overwhelmed by academic demands. They have started to challenge their thoughts of worthlessness and have developed a more balanced perspective on their abilities.
intermediate_belief: If I don't meet my high standards, then I am not good enough, and I must work harder to prove my worth.
intermediate_belief_depression: If I can't control my emotions, then I am failing and will never succeed.
coping_strategies: The client uses physical sensations like splashing water on their face and stepping outside to reset their thoughts and emotions. They also engage in physical activities like running to disrupt negative cognitive patterns.
situation: Working on a PhD project and concerned about its progress.
auto_thought: I'm going to lose control if I don't make progress soon.
behavior: The client splashes water on their face and steps outside to feel the cold air, using these physical sensations to regain control over their thoughts.
